20	\section{Setup and motivation}
21	\label{sec:flatstrat_setup}
22	
23	The construction of the Quot scheme \(\Quot_{\F/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) in Nitsure's exposition
24	of Grothendieck's existence theorem (\cite{nitsure-hilbert-quot}, \S5) proceeds by
25	embedding the functor of points of \(\Quot\) into a Grassmannian via Castelnuovo--Mumford
26	regularity, then cutting out the locus of \emph{quotients with flat fibres of the
27	prescribed Hilbert polynomial} by a closed-subscheme condition.  Cutting out this
28	locus uses, as a primary technical input, the existence of the
29	\emph{flattening stratification} of a coherent sheaf \(\F\) on a projective
30	\(S\)-scheme: a finite collection of locally-closed subschemes of \(S\) on each of which
31	the restriction of \(\F\) becomes flat.
32	
33	This chapter packages the Nitsure~\S4 flattening-stratification theorem as the
34	foundational A.2.a building block. The Quot-scheme construction itself (A.2.b),
35	the relative Picard functor (A.1.c), and the assembly of the Picard scheme
36	representability theorem on a smooth proper curve (A.2.c) live in separate sibling
37	chapters.
38	
39	We work throughout with a noetherian base scheme \(S\), a projective morphism
40	\(\pi : X \to S\), and a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module \(\F\). The conclusion of Nitsure's
41	theorem covers an arbitrary noetherian base with a projective \(X \to S\), which
42	specialises directly to the Route~A consumer setting where \(S = T\) is an arbitrary
43	\(k\)-scheme, \(X = C \times_k T\) for \(C\) a smooth proper curve over the base field
44	\(k\), and \(\F\) is a coherent quotient sheaf produced by the Grassmannian embedding
45	of the Quot functor (cf.\ \ below).
46	
47	\section{Coherent flatness over a base}
48	\label{sec:coh_flat}
49	
50	\begin{definition}
51	
52	\leanok
53	[Coherent sheaf flat over the base]
54	  \label{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}
55	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat}
56	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(f : X \to S\) a morphism of finite type, and
57	  \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module.  We say \(\F\) is \emph{flat over \(S\)}
58	  (equivalently, \(\F\) is \emph{\(f\)-flat}, or \emph{\(S\)-flat}) if for every
59	  point \(x \in X\) the stalk \(\F_x\) is a flat \(\OO_{S, f(x)}\)-module, where the
60	  \(\OO_{S, f(x)}\)-module structure is given by the composite
61	  \(\OO_{S, f(x)} \to \OO_{X, x} \to \End(\F_x)\).
62	  \textit{Source: cf.\ [Stacks Project], tag 00HB (flat modules); [Nitsure], \S4
63	  passim.}
64	  Equivalently (after pulling back to an affine open \(U = \Spec A \subseteq S\) and
65	  an affine open \(V = \Spec B \subseteq f^{-1}(U)\) with \(\F|_V = \widetilde{M}\)
66	  for \(M\) a finite \(B\)-module), \(\F|_V\) is \(\OO_U\)-flat in the sense that \(M\) is a
67	  flat \(A\)-module (with the \(A\)-module structure coming from the ring map
68	  \(A \to B\)).
69	\end{definition}
70	
71	\begin{remark}
72	\label{rem:coh_flat_pullback}
73	  The condition is stable under base change: for any morphism \(g : S' \to S\),
74	  pulling back along the cartesian square
75	  \[
76	    \begin{tikzcd}
77	      X' \ar[r, "g'"] \ar[d, "f'"'] & X \ar[d, "f"] \\
78	      S' \ar[r, "g"]                & S
79	    \end{tikzcd}
80	  \]
81	  yields a coherent \(\OO_{X'}\)-module \(\F' := (g')^* \F\) which is \(S'\)-flat
82	  whenever \(\F\) is \(S\)-flat.  This is the routine cancellation of tensor-product
83	  associativity at the level of stalks. The converse fails in general.
84	\end{remark}
85	
86	\section{Generic flatness}
87	\label{sec:generic_flatness}
88	
89	The construction of the flattening stratification proceeds by Noetherian
90	induction on \(S\). The induction step relies on the following classical
91	\emph{generic flatness} theorem of Grothendieck: over a noetherian integral
92	base, a coherent sheaf is flat over a non-empty open subscheme. Algebraically,
93	this is the assertion that a finitely generated module over a finite-type
94	algebra over a noetherian domain is generically free after inverting a single
95	non-zero element of the base ring.
96	
97	\begin{theorem}
98	[Generic flatness, algebraic form]
99	  \label{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}
100	  
101	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness''
102	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1711-L1716).
103	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
104	  % "Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain,
105	  %  and \(B\) a finite type \(A\) algebra. Let \(M\) be a finite \(B\)-module.
106	  %  Then there exists an \(f\in A\), \(f\ne 0\), such that
107	  %  the localisation \(M_f\) is a free module over \(A_f\)."
108	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness''.}
109	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain, \(B\) a finite-type \(A\)-algebra, and \(M\) a finite
110	  \(B\)-module. Then there exists \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that the localisation
111	  \(M_f\) is a free \(A_f\)-module.
112	\end{theorem}
113	
114	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Over the quotient field \(K\) of \(A\), the \(K\)-algebra
115	% \(B_K = K\otimes _AB\) is of finite type, and \(M_K = K\otimes_AM\) is a
116	% finite module over \(B_K\). Let \(n\) be the dimension of the support
117	% of \(M_K\) over \(\spec B_K\). We argue by induction on \(n\), starting
118	% with \(n=-1\) which is the case when \(M_K =0\). [...]
119	% Now let \(n\ge 0\), and let the lemma be proved for smaller values.
120	% As \(B\) is noetherian and \(M\) is assumed to be a finite \(B\)-module,
121	% there exists a finite filtration
122	% \(0=M_0 \subset \ldots  \subset M_r =M\)
123	% where each \(M_i\) is a \(B\)-submodule of \(M\) such that for each \(i\ge 1\)
124	% the quotient module \(M_i/M_{i-1}\) is isomorphic to \(B/\pp_i\) for
125	% some prime ideal \(\pp_i\) in \(B\). [...] By Noether normalisation lemma,
126	% there exist elements \(b_1,\ldots,b_n \in B\), such that \(K\otimes_AB\) is
127	% finite over its subalgebra \(K[b_1,\ldots, b_n]\) [...]"
128	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1719-L1772; full
129	% proof omitted for length, structural steps restated below in project notation.)
130	\begin{proof}
131	  
132	  Following [Nitsure], \S4: pass to the fraction field \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\) and
133	  set \(B_K = K \otimes_A B\), \(M_K = K \otimes_A M\), \(n := \dim \mathrm{supp}_{\Spec B_K} M_K\).
134	  Argue by induction on \(n\), with base case \(n = -1\) (i.e.\ \(M_K = 0\)): each
135	  generator of \(M\) is annihilated by some non-zero element of \(A\); taking the
136	  product of finitely many annihilators yields \(f \in A\) with \(f M = 0\), hence
137	  \(M_f = 0\) is trivially free.  For \(n \ge 0\), a prime filtration of \(M\) as a
138	  finite \(B\)-module
139	  \[
140	    0 = M_0 \subset M_1 \subset \cdots \subset M_r = M
141	  \]
142	  with successive quotients \(M_i / M_{i-1} \cong B / \mathfrak{p}_i\) reduces the
143	  problem (via the splicing fact: \(M_{f'f''}\) is \(A_{f'f''}\)-free when \(M'_{f'}\)
144	  and \(M''_{f''}\) are free) to the case \(M = B/\mathfrak{p}\) for a prime
145	  \(\mathfrak{p} \subset B\), then to the case \(B\) a domain and \(M = B\).
146	  Noether normalisation applied to \(B_K / K\) yields algebraically independent
147	  \(b_1, \dots, b_n \in B\) with \(B_K\) finite over \(K[b_1, \dots, b_n]\); choosing
148	  \(g \ne 0\) to clear denominators of integrality equations, \(B_g\) is finite over
149	  \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\). Let \(m\) be the generic rank: the short exact sequence
150	  \[
151	    0 \to A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]^{\oplus m} \to B_g \to T \to 0
152	  \]
153	  has \(T\) a finite torsion module over \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\), so $\dim
154	  \mathrm{supp}_{B_K} (K \otimes_{A_g} T) < n$.  The inductive hypothesis
155	  yields \(h \ne 0\) in \(A\) with \(T_h\) free over \(A_{gh}\); set \(f := gh\).
156	\end{proof}
157	
158	\begin{theorem}
159	
160	\leanok
161	[Generic flatness, geometric form]
162	  \label{thm:generic_flatness}
163	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness}
164	  
165	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''
166	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1781-L1787).
167	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
168	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian and integral scheme.
169	  %  Let \(p: X\to S\) be a finite type morphism, and let \(\F\) be a
170	  %  coherent sheaf of \({\mathcal O}_X\)-modules. Then there exists a non-empty
171	  %  open subscheme \(U\subset S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\)
172	  %  to \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is flat over \({\mathcal O}_U\)."
173	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''.}
174	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian integral scheme, \(p : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
175	  and \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module. Then there exists a non-empty open
176	  subscheme \(U \subseteq S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\) to
177	  \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is \(\OO_U\)-flat.
178	\end{theorem}
179	
180	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The above theorem has the following consequence, which
181	% follows by restricting attention to a non-empty affine open subscheme of \(S\)."
182	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1776-L1777. The
183	% Nitsure proof is the single sentence quoted: deduce the geometric form from
184	% Theorem~REF by restricting to a non-empty
185	% affine open of \(S\), where the situation reduces to the algebraic statement.)
186	\begin{proof}
187	  
188	  Restrict to a non-empty affine open \(\Spec A \subseteq S\) where \(A\) is a
189	  noetherian domain. The morphism \(p\) is of finite type, so locally on \(X\)
190	  above this affine \(\F\) is the sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\) for \(M\) a finite module
191	  over a finite-type \(A\)-algebra \(B\). Theorem~REF
192	  produces \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), with \(M_f\) free over \(A_f\) on each such patch;
193	  shrinking to the basic open \(D(f) \subseteq \Spec A\) globalises by patching
194	  finitely many such opens (using noetherianity of \(X\) above the affine).
195	\end{proof}
196	
197	\section{Existence of the flattening stratification}
198	\label{sec:flatstrat_exists}
199	
200	\begin{theorem}
201	
202	\leanok
203	[Existence of the flattening stratification]
204	  \label{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}
205	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification}
206	  
207	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``flattening stratification''
208	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1811-L1841).
209	  % Parallel pointer: [Stacks Project], tag 052H (verbatim text not yet
210	  % retrieved into a local references/ file; see ``Notes for Plan Agent'').
211	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
212	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, and let
213	  %  \(\F\) be a coherent sheaf on the projective space \(\PP^n_S\)
214	  %  over \(S\). Then the set \(I\) of Hilbert polynomials of
215	  %  restrictions of \(\F\) to fibers of \(\PP^n_S\to S\)
216	  %  is a finite set. Moreover, for each \(f\in I\) there exist
217	  %  a locally closed subscheme \(S_f\) of \(S\),
218	  %  such that the following conditions are satisfied.
219	  %
220	  %  \textbf{(i) Point-set: } The underlying set \(|S_f|\) of \(S_f\) consists of
221	  %  all points \(s\in S\) where the Hilbert polynomial of the
222	  %  restriction of \(\F\) to \(\PP^n_s\) is \(f\). In particular,
223	  %  the subsets \(|S_f|\subset |S|\) are disjoint,
224	  %  and their set-theoretic union is \(|S|\).
225	  %
226	  %  \textbf{(ii) Universal property: }
227	  %  Let \(S'=\coprod S_f\) be the coproduct of the \(S_f\), and let
228	  %  \(i:S'\to S\) be the morphism induced by the inclusions
229	  %  \(S_f\hra S\). Then the sheaf \(i^*(\F)\) on \(\PP^n_{S'}\)
230	  %  is flat over \(S'\). Moreover, \(i:S'\to S\) has the universal property that
231	  %  for any morphism \(\varphi :T\to S\) the pullback \(\varphi^*(\F)\) on
232	  %  \(\PP^n_T\) is flat over \(T\) if and only if \(\varphi\) factors through
233	  %  \(i:S'\to S\).  The subscheme \(S_f\) is
234	  %  uniquely determined by the polynomial \(f\).
235	  %
236	  %  \textbf{(iii) Closure of strata: }
237	  %  Let the set \(I\) of Hilbert polynomials be given a total
238	  %  ordering, defined by putting \(f<g\) whenever \(f(n) < g(n)\) for all
239	  %  \(n \gg  0\). Then the closure in \(S\) of the subset \(|S_f|\)
240	  %  is contained in the union of all \(|S_g|\) where \(f\le g\)."
241	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``flattening stratification'';
242	  cf.\ [Stacks Project], tag 052H.}
243	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and \(\F\) a coherent sheaf on the relative
244	  projective space \(\PP^n_S\) over \(S\).  Then:
245	  \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)]
246	    \item The set \(I\) of Hilbert polynomials
247	      \(P_s(m) = \chi(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) that arise as \(s\) ranges over \(S\) is
248	      finite.
249	    \item For each \(f \in I\) there exists a locally-closed subscheme
250	      \(S_f \hookrightarrow S\) whose underlying set \(|S_f|\) consists of exactly
251	      those \(s \in S\) with \(P_s = f\).  The collection \(\{|S_f|\}_{f \in I}\) is
252	      pairwise disjoint with set-theoretic union \(|S|\).  Forming the coproduct
253	      \(S' := \coprod_{f \in I} S_f\) and the morphism \(i : S' \to S\) assembled
254	      from the inclusions \(S_f \hookrightarrow S\), the pullback \(i^* \F\) on
255	      \(\PP^n_{S'}\) is flat over \(S'\), and \(i : S' \to S\) is universal with this
256	      property: for any morphism \(\varphi : T \to S\) such that \(\varphi^* \F\) on
257	      \(\PP^n_T\) is flat over \(T\), \(\varphi\) factors uniquely through \(i\).
258	      Each \(S_f\) is uniquely determined by \(f\).
259	    \item Order \(I\) by \(f < g \iff f(n) < g(n)\) for all \(n \gg 0\). Then the
260	      closure of \(|S_f|\) in \(|S|\) is contained in \(\bigcup_{g \ge f} |S_g|\).
261	  \end{enumerate}
262	\end{theorem}
263	
264	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "It is enough to prove the theorem for open subschemes of
265	% $S$ which cover $S$, as the resulting strata will then glue together by their
266	% universal property. [...] \textbf{General case: } We now allow the integer
267	% $n$ to be arbitrary. The idea of the proof is as follows: We show the
268	% existence of a stratification of $S$ which is a `g.c.d.' of the flattening
269	% stratifications for direct images $\pi_*\F(i)$ for all $i \ge N$ for some
270	% integer $N$ (where the flattening stratifications for $\pi_*\F(i)$ exist by
271	% case $n=0$ which we have treated above). This is the desired flattening
272	% stratification of $\F$ over $S$, as follows from Lemma REF."
273	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1844-L1900; the
274	% full body L1844-L2090 is distributed across the sub-lemmas below, each of
275	% which carries its own verbatim % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF.)
276	\begin{proof}
277	  \uses{def:coherent_sheaf_flat, lem:flat_locus_open, lem:nonflat_locus_proper,
278	    lem:noetherian_induction_strata, thm:generic_flatness}
279	  Following [Nitsure], \S4, the construction reduces to the special case
280	  \(n = 0\), assembled over the relative twists \(\pi_* \F(N+i)\).  As the
281	  argument is local on \(S\) (the strata glue by their universal property, part
282	  (ii)), and as the three conclusions are proved through the sub-lemmas below,
283	  we assemble them in the order (i), (ii), (iii).
284	
285	  \emph{Part (i): finiteness of the Hilbert-polynomial set.}
286	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:nonflat_locus_proper}, Noetherian induction on \(S\) (whose
287	  inductive step invokes generic flatness, Theorem~\ref{thm:generic_flatness},
288	  on the integral locally-closed strata cut out by the irreducible-component
289	  decomposition of \(S\)) produces finitely many reduced, pairwise disjoint,
290	  locally-closed subschemes \(V_i \hookrightarrow S\) set-theoretically covering
291	  \(|S|\) on each of which \(\F|_{\PP^n_{V_i}}\) is flat over \(\OO_{V_i}\) in
292	  the sense of Definition~\ref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}.  On a flat family the
293	  Hilbert polynomial \(P_s(m) = \chi(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) is locally constant, so
294	  it takes finitely many values on each quasi-compact \(V_i\); the finite union
295	  over the finitely many \(V_i\) shows that the set \(I\) of Hilbert polynomials
296	  occurring on \(S\) is finite.
297	
298	  \emph{Part (ii): existence of the strata and their universal property.}
299	  Working over the flat pieces \(V_i\) of part (i), semicontinuity
300	  (cohomology-and-base-change) yields a uniform integer \(N\) such that, for all
301	  \(s \in S\) and \(m \ge N\), the higher cohomologies \(H^r(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\)
302	  vanish for \(r \ge 1\) and the base-change homomorphism
303	  \((\pi_* \F(m))|_s \to H^0(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) is an isomorphism.  With this
304	  \(N\) fixed, Lemma~\ref{lem:noetherian_induction_strata} applies the \(n = 0\)
305	  stratification mechanism (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_locus_open}) successively to the
306	  coherent sheaves \(E_i = \pi_* \F(N+i)\) on \(S\) for \(i = 0, \dots, n\),
307	  refining stratum by stratum.  After \(n+1\) steps this produces a finite
308	  family of locally-closed subschemes \(W_{e_0, \dots, e_n} \subseteq S\),
309	  universal for the property that each \(E_i\) pulls back to a locally-free sheaf
310	  of constant rank \(e_i\); re-indexing the tuple \((e_0, \dots, e_n)\) by the
311	  unique numerical polynomial \(f\) of degree \(\le n\) with \(f(N+i) = e_i\)
312	  gives the family \(\{W_f\}_{f \in I}\).  At any \(s \in W_f\) the base-change
313	  isomorphism and the cohomology vanishing force \(P_s = f\) (since \(P_s\) has
314	  degree \(\le n\) and is determined by its values at \(N, \dots, N+n\)), so
315	  \(|W_f| = \{s : P_s = f\}\); these subsets are disjoint with union \(|S|\).
316	  The required locally-closed subscheme \(S_f \subseteq W_f\) is the closed
317	  subscheme cut out by the further local-freeness conditions
318	  ``\(\pi_* \F(N+i)|_{W_f}\) is locally free of rank \(f(N+i)\) for all
319	  \(i \ge 0\)'' (a chain of coherent ideal sheaves that stabilises by
320	  Noetherianity), and \(\F|_{\PP^n_{S_f}}\) is flat over \(S_f\)
321	  (Definition~\ref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}) because the graded module
322	  \(\bigoplus_{i \gg 0} \pi_* \F(i)|_{S_f}\) is locally free.  Forming
323	  \(S' = \coprod_{f \in I} S_f\) and the assembled morphism \(i : S' \to S\),
324	  the pullback \(i^* \F\) is \(S'\)-flat; and any \(\varphi : T \to S\) with
325	  \(\varphi^* \F\) flat over \(T\) has locally constant Hilbert polynomial, hence
326	  decomposes \(T = \coprod_f T_f\) into the open-closed loci where the polynomial
327	  is \(f\), each factoring uniquely through the corresponding \(S_f\) by the
328	  local-freeness universal property of Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_locus_open}.  This
329	  gives the unique factorisation through \(i\), and shows each \(S_f\) is
330	  uniquely determined by \(f\).
331	
332	  \emph{Part (iii): closure of strata.}
333	  Order \(I\) by \(f < g \iff f(n) < g(n)\) for all \(n \gg 0\).  Since each
334	  \(S_f\) is a stratum of the \(n = 0\) flattening stratification applied to a
335	  single direct image \(\pi_* \F(p)\) for \(p \gg 0\) separating all polynomials
336	  in \(I\), the closure-of-strata assertion reduces to the upper-semicontinuity
337	  statement already established in the \(n = 0\) case
338	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_locus_open}): the closure of \(|S_f|\) in \(|S|\) is
339	  contained in \(\bigcup_{g \ge f} |S_g|\).
340	\end{proof}
341	
342	The proof is long; we split it into sub-lemmas below.  The first sub-lemma is
343	the special case \(n = 0\) (when \(\PP^n_S = S\) and \(\F\) is just a coherent sheaf
344	on \(S\)), which already contains the flat-locus-as-locally-closed-subscheme
345	mechanism. The remaining inputs are (A) the Noetherian-induction reduction to
346	the flat case via generic flatness; (B) uniform vanishing of higher cohomology
347	of \(\F(m)\) for \(m \gg 0\) on \(\F_{V_i}\) for the flat pieces; and (C) base-change
348	isomorphisms for the direct images \(\pi_* \F(m)\), which allow comparing
349	strata for different twists.
350	
351	\begin{lemma}
352	[Flat locus on \(S\) for a coherent sheaf is a locally-closed stratification]
353	  \label{lem:flat_locus_open}
354	  
355	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``flattening stratification'', Special case
356	  % \(n=0\) (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
357	  % L1849-L1885).
358	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
359	  % "\textbf{Special case: } Let \(n=0\), so that \(\PP^n_S=S\).

361	  %  will mean the pull-back of \(\F\) to the subscheme
362	  %  \(\spec \kappa(s)\) [...]. The Hilbert polynomial of the
363	  %  restriction of \(\F\) to the fiber over \(s\) is the degree \(0\)
364	  %  polynomial \(e \in \Q[\lambda]\), where \(e = \dim_{\kappa(s)}\F|_s\).
365	  %

367	  %  to give a set of generators for \(F|_U\). Repeating this argument,
368	  %  we see that there exists a smaller neighbourhood \(V\) of \(s\) in which
369	  %  there is a right-exact sequence
370	  %  ${\mathcal O}_V^{\oplus m} \stackrel{\psi}{\to} {\mathcal O}_V^{\oplus e}
371	  %  \stackrel{\phi}{\to} \F \to 0$.
372	  %  Let \(I_e\subset {\mathcal O}_V\) be the ideal sheaf

375	  %  Let \(V_e\) be the closed subscheme of \(V\) defined by \(I_e\).

377	  %  \(f: T\to V\), the pull-back sequence [...]
378	  %  is exact, by right-exactness of tensor products.
379	  %  Hence the pull-back \(f^*\F\) is a locally free \({\mathcal O}_T\)-module of

381	  %  \(f\) factors via the subscheme \(V_e\hra V\) defined by the vanishing of all
382	  %  entries \(\psi_{i,j}\).
383	  %  Thus we have proved assertions \textbf{(i)} and \textbf{(ii)} of the theorem.
384	  %
385	  %  As the rank of the matrix \((\psi_{i,j})\) is lower semi-continuous,
386	  %  it follows that the function \(e\) is upper semi-continuous, which
387	  %  proves the assertion \textbf{(iii)} of the theorem, completing its proof
388	  %  when \(n=0\)."
389	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (special case \(n=0\) of the main theorem).}
390	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(\F\) be a coherent \(\OO_S\)-module.
391	  For each integer \(e \ge 0\), there exists a locally-closed subscheme
392	  \(S_e \hookrightarrow S\) whose underlying set is
393	  \(\{s \in S : \dim_{\kappa(s)} \F|_s = e\}\). The family \(\{S_e\}_e\) is a
394	  finite, mutually disjoint, set-theoretically covering stratification of \(S\),
395	  and a morphism \(f : T \to S\) pulls back \(\F\) to a locally-free
396	  \(\OO_T\)-module of rank \(e\) if and only if \(f\) factors through \(S_e\).  The
397	  function \(s \mapsto \dim_{\kappa(s)} \F|_s\) is upper-semicontinuous on \(S\).
398	\end{lemma}
399	
400	\begin{proof}
401	  
402	  By [Nitsure], \S4 (verbatim above): for \(s \in S\), Nakayama produces a
403	  neighbourhood \(V\) of \(s\) and a right-exact local presentation
404	  \(\OO_V^{\oplus m} \xrightarrow{\psi} \OO_V^{\oplus e} \to \F|_V \to 0\) where
405	  \(e = \dim_{\kappa(s)} \F|_s\).  The closed subscheme \(V_e \hookrightarrow V\)
406	  defined by the ideal sheaf generated by the matrix entries of \(\psi\)
407	  represents the locus where \(\F\) becomes locally free of rank \(e\): by
408	  right-exactness of tensor product, a base change \(f : T \to V\) has
409	  \(f^* \F\) locally free of rank \(e\) iff \(f^* \psi = 0\), iff \(f\) factors
410	  through \(V_e\).  Glueing the \(V_e\) over the cover of \(S\) by such
411	  neighbourhoods (and intersecting with \(V \setminus \bigcup_{e' > e} V_{e'}\)
412	  to get the strictly-equal-to-\(e\) stratum) produces the locally-closed
413	  \(S_e\). Upper-semicontinuity of \(e\) follows because the rank of \(\psi\) is
414	  lower-semicontinuous.
415	\end{proof}
416	
417	\begin{lemma}
418	[Noetherian induction reduction to the flat case]
419	  \label{lem:nonflat_locus_proper}
420	  
421	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``flattening stratification'', General case
422	  % opening paragraphs (read from
423	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1888-L1929).
424	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
425	  % "\textbf{General case: } We now allow the integer \(n\) to be arbitrary.
426	  %  The idea of the proof is as follows:
427	  %  We show the existence of a stratification
428	  %  of \(S\) which is a `g.c.d.' of the flattening
429	  %  stratifications for direct images \(\pi_*\F(i)\) for all \(i \ge N\) for some
430	  %  integer \(N\) [...].
431	  %
432	  %  As \(S\) is noetherian, it is a finite union of irreducible components,
433	  %  and these are closed in \(S\).
434	  %  Let \(Y\) be an irreducible component of \(S\), and let \(U\) be the
435	  %  non-empty open subset of \(Y\) which consists of all points which do not
436	  %  lie on any other irreducible component of \(S\).
437	  %  Let \(U\) be given the reduced subscheme
438	  %  structure. Note that this makes \(U\) an integral scheme, which is a
439	  %  locally closed subscheme of \(S\).
440	  %  By Theorem REF on generic flatness,
441	  %  \(U\) has a non-empty open subscheme \(V\) such that
442	  %  the restriction of \(\F\) to \(\PP^n_V\) is flat over \({\mathcal O}_V\).
443	  %  Now repeating the argument with \(S\) replaced by its reduced
444	  %  closed subscheme \(S-V\),
445	  %  it follows by noetherian induction on \(S\) that there exist
446	  %  finitely many reduced, locally closed, mutually disjoint subschemes
447	  %  \(V_i\) of \(S\) such that set-theoretically
448	  %  \(|S|\) is the union of the \(|V_i|\) and
449	  %  the restriction of \(\F\) to \(\PP^n_{V_i}\) is flat over \({\mathcal O}_{V_i}\).
450	  %  As each \(V_i\) is a noetherian scheme, and as the Hilbert polynomials
451	  %  are locally constant for a flat family of sheaves, it follows
452	  %  that only finitely many polynomials occur in \(V_i\)
453	  %  in the family of Hilbert polynomials \(P_s(m) = \chi(\PP^n_s,\F_s(m))\)
454	  %  as \(s\) varies over points of \(V_i\). This allows us to conclude
455	  %  the following:
456	  %
457	  %  \textbf{(A) } Only finitely many distinct Hilbert polynomials
458	  %  \(P_s(m) = \chi(\PP^n_s,\F_s(m))\) occur, as \(s\)
459	  %  varies over all of \(S\)."
460	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (general case, reduction by Noetherian induction).}
461	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(\pi : \PP^n_S \to S\) the relative projective
462	  space, and \(\F\) a coherent sheaf on \(\PP^n_S\).  Then there exist finitely many
463	  reduced, locally-closed, pairwise disjoint subschemes \(V_i \hookrightarrow S\)
464	  whose set-theoretic union is \(|S|\) and such that \(\F|_{\PP^n_{V_i}}\) is
465	  flat over \(\OO_{V_i}\) for each \(i\). Consequently, only finitely many
466	  Hilbert polynomials \(P_s(m) = \chi(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) occur as \(s\) varies
467	  over \(S\).
468	\end{lemma}
469	
470	\begin{proof}
471	  
472	  By [Nitsure], \S4: \(S\) being noetherian, decompose \(|S| = \bigcup Y_j\) into
473	  finitely many irreducible components, each closed.  For an irreducible
474	  component \(Y\), let \(U \subseteq Y\) be the open subset not lying on any other
475	  component, given the reduced subscheme structure (an integral, locally-closed
476	  subscheme of \(S\)).  By Theorem~REF applied to
477	  \(\F|_{\PP^n_U}\), there is a non-empty open \(V \subseteq U\) over which \(\F\) is
478	  flat.  Replace \(S\) by the reduced closed complement \(S \setminus V\) and
479	  repeat. Noetherianity of \(S\) terminates the induction in finitely many
480	  steps, producing the \(V_i\).  On each \(V_i\) the Hilbert polynomial is locally
481	  constant (flat families have locally constant Hilbert polynomial), hence
482	  takes finitely many values on the noetherian (hence quasi-compact) scheme
483	  \(V_i\); the finite union of finite sets is finite.
484	\end{proof}

486	\begin{lemma}
487	[Assembly of the strata by Noetherian induction]
488	  \label{lem:noetherian_induction_strata}
489	  
490	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``flattening stratification'', Assembly via
491	  % direct images \(E_i = \pi_*\F(N+i)\) (read from

496	  %  \(E_i = \pi_*\F(N+i)\mbox{ for } i=0,\ldots,n.\)

498	  %  \(n\) of \(\PP^n_S\) is \(0\)) to the sheaf \(E_0\) on \(\PP^0_S=S\), we get

508	  %  such that for any morphism \(f:T\to S\) the pull-back
509	  %  \(f^*E_i\) for \(i=0,\ldots,n\) is a locally free \({\mathcal O}_T\)-modules of
510	  %  of constant rank \(e_i\) if and only if \(f\) factors
511	  %  via \(W_{e_0,\ldots,e_n} \hra S\). [...]
512	  %  This shows that at any point \(s\in W_f\), the Hilbert polynomial
513	  %  \(P_s(m)\) equals \(f\). The desired locally closed subscheme \(S_f \subset S\),
514	  %  whose existence is asserted by the theorem,
515	  %  will turn out to be a certain closed subscheme \(S_f \subset W_f\) whose
516	  %  underlying subset is all of \(|W_f|\). [...]
517	  %  As \(S_f\) is the flattening stratification for \(\pi_*\F(p)\),
518	  %  the property (iii) of the theorem follows from the corresponding property in
519	  %  the case \(n=0\), applied to the sheaf \(\pi_*\F(p)\) on \(S\)."
520	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (assembly step combining the \(n=0\) special case
521	  applied to direct images \(\pi_*\F(N+i)\)).}
522	  Let \(S\), \(\pi : \PP^n_S \to S\), \(\F\) be as in
523	  Theorem~REF.  There exists an integer
524	  \(N \ge 0\) such that for every \(s \in S\) the higher cohomologies
525	  \(H^r(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) vanish for all \(r \ge 1\), \(m \ge N\), and the base-change
526	  homomorphism \((\pi_* \F(m))|_s \to H^0(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) is an isomorphism
527	  for \(m \ge N\), \(s \in S\).  Iterating the \(n=0\) stratification
528	  (Lemma~REF) on the coherent sheaves \(E_i := \pi_* \F(N+i)\)
529	  for \(i = 0, \dots, n\) produces a finite collection of locally-closed
530	  subschemes \(W_{e_0, \dots, e_n} \subseteq S\) indexed by tuples
531	  \((e_0, \dots, e_n) \in \Z^{n+1}\). Re-indexing each tuple by the unique
532	  numerical polynomial \(f \in \Q[\lambda]\) of degree \(\le n\) with
533	  \(f(N+i) = e_i\) gives a finite stratification \(\{W_f\}\) of \(S\), and the
534	  locally-closed subschemes \(S_f \subseteq W_f\) of
535	  Theorem~REF are obtained by further
536	  intersecting with the closed-subscheme conditions that
537	  \(\pi_* \F(N+i)|_{W_f}\) be locally free of rank \(f(N+i)\) for all \(i \ge 0\)
538	  (terminating by Noetherianity).

540	
541	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: full proof is the long body of [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem
542	% ``flattening stratification'', spanning L1844-L2090 (~250 lines: special
543	% case, Noetherian induction, statements (A)/(B)/(C), then the assembly via
544	% direct images and the closed-subscheme construction inside each \(W_f\)).
545	% Verbatim retrieved per-sub-lemma above; the structural restatement below in
546	% project notation is the canonical Nitsure-style restated proof.
547	\begin{proof}
548	  
549	  By Lemma~REF, the family of Hilbert polynomials
550	  \(\{P_s\}\) on \(S\) is finite, and there is a finite stratification
551	  \(\{V_i\}\) of \(S\) on each of which \(\F\) is flat with locally-constant
552	  Hilbert polynomial.  Apply semi-continuity (cohomology and base change,
553	  [Hartshorne], III.12 / [Stacks Project], tag 02KH for the \(H^0\)-version) on
554	  the noetherian \(V_i\) to obtain a uniform integer \(N_1\) with
555	  \(R^r \pi_* \F(m) = 0\) for \(r \ge 1\), \(m \ge N_1\), and \(H^r(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m)) = 0\)
556	  for all \(s \in S\), \(r \ge 1\), \(m \ge N_1\) (statement (B) of Nitsure's proof).
557	  Choose \(r_i \ge N_1\) on each \(V_i\) such that the base-change comparison
558	  \((\pi_* \F(m))|_{V_i} \to (\pi_i)_* \F_{V_i}(m)\) is an isomorphism for
559	  \(m \ge r_i\); composing with the cohomology-and-base-change isomorphism
560	  \(((\pi_i)_* \F_{V_i}(m))|_s \xrightarrow{\sim} H^0(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) for
561	  the flat family on \(V_i\) yields uniform \(N = \max r_i\) such that
562	  \((\pi_* \F(m))|_s \to H^0(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) is an isomorphism for \(m \ge N\)
563	  and all \(s \in S\) (statement (C)).
564	
565	  Apply Lemma~REF sequentially to the coherent sheaves
566	  \(E_i = \pi_* \F(N+i)\) on \(S\) for \(i = 0, \dots, n\): \(E_0\) produces a
567	  stratification \(\{W_{e_0}\}\) of \(S\); on each \(W_{e_0}\) apply the lemma to
568	  \(E_1|_{W_{e_0}}\) to refine to \(\{W_{e_0, e_1}\}\), and continue. After \(n+1\)
569	  steps the \(W_{e_0, \dots, e_n}\) form a finite locally-closed stratification
570	  of \(S\) universal for the property ``each \(E_i\) pulls back to a locally-free
571	  \(\OO_T\)-module of constant rank \(e_i\)''.  Re-index \((e_0, \dots, e_n)\) by the
572	  unique numerical polynomial \(f\) of degree \(\le n\) with \(f(N+i) = e_i\) to get
573	  the family \(\{W_f\}\) indexed by polynomials.  At any \(s \in W_f\), statement
574	  (C) and vanishing (B) force \(P_s = f\) (since \(P_s\) has degree \(\le n\) and is
575	  determined by its values at \(N, N+1, \dots, N+n\)).
576	
577	  The Hilbert-polynomial-coincidence locus \(|W_f|\) may have non-flat scheme
578	  structure; refine to \(S_f \subseteq W_f\) by intersecting with the
579	  closed-subscheme conditions ``\(\pi_* \F(N+i)|_{W_f}\) is locally-free of rank
580	  \(f(N+i)\)'' for all \(i \ge 0\).  These conditions are cut out by an increasing
581	  chain of coherent ideal sheaves \(I_0 \subseteq I_0 + I_1 \subseteq \cdots\) on
582	  \(W_f\); Noetherianity terminates the chain in finitely many steps, defining a
583	  coherent ideal sheaf \(I\) and a closed subscheme \(S_f \subseteq W_f\) with
584	  \(|S_f| = |W_f|\) on which \(\pi_* \F(N+i)\) is locally-free of rank \(f(N+i)\)
585	  for all \(i \ge 0\). The base-change-without-flatness lemma (cf.\ [Nitsure],
586	  \S3) yields some \(N' \ge N\) such that for \(i \ge N'\),
587	  \((\pi_* \F(i))|_{S_f} \to (\pi_{S_f})_* \F_{S_f}(i)\) is an isomorphism, hence
588	  the graded \(A\)-module \(\bigoplus_{i \ge N'} (\pi_{S_f})_* \F_{S_f}(i)\) is
589	  locally-free on \(S_f\), hence \(\F_{S_f}\) is flat over \(S_f\). The
590	  closure-of-strata property (iii) reduces to the corresponding property in
591	  the \(n=0\) case for \(\pi_* \F(p)\) on \(S\) at any \(p \ge N\) separating all
592	  Hilbert polynomials.
593	\end{proof}

595	\section{Universal property of the strata}
596	\label{sec:flatstrat_universal}
597	
598	\begin{theorem}
599	
600	\leanok
601	[Universal property of the flattening stratification]
602	  \label{thm:flattening_stratification_universal}
603	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification_universal}
604	  
605	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``flattening stratification'', part (ii)
606	  % universal property
607	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1826-L1834).
608	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
609	  % "\textbf{(ii) Universal property: }
610	  %  Let \(S'=\coprod S_f\) be the coproduct of the \(S_f\), and let
611	  %  \(i:S'\to S\) be the morphism induced by the inclusions
612	  %  \(S_f\hra S\). Then the sheaf \(i^*(\F)\) on \(\PP^n_{S'}\)
613	  %  is flat over \(S'\). Moreover, \(i:S'\to S\) has the universal property that
614	  %  for any morphism \(\varphi :T\to S\) the pullback \(\varphi^*(\F)\) on
615	  %  \(\PP^n_T\) is flat over \(T\) if and only if \(\varphi\) factors through
616	  %  \(i:S'\to S\).  The subscheme \(S_f\) is
617	  %  uniquely determined by the polynomial \(f\)."
618	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (part (ii) of the main theorem); cf.\ [Stacks
619	  Project], tag 052H.}
620	  Let \(S\), \(\F\), and \(\{S_f\}_{f \in I}\) be as in
621	  Theorem~REF.  Set
622	  \(S' := \coprod_{f \in I} S_f\) with the coproduct morphism
623	  \(i : S' \to S\) assembled from the locally-closed inclusions.  Then \(i^* \F\)
624	  is flat over \(S'\), and the pair \((S', i)\) is universal: for any morphism
625	  \(\varphi : T \to S\) of noetherian schemes (one may take \(T\) arbitrary, the
626	  Noetherian hypothesis on \(S\) being preserved), the pullback
627	  \(\varphi^* \F\) on \(\PP^n_T\) is \(T\)-flat if and only if \(\varphi\) factors
628	  uniquely through \(i\). In particular each \(S_f\) is uniquely determined by \(f\).
629	\end{theorem}
630	
631	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "We now show that the morphism \(\coprod_f S_f \,\to \,S\)
632	% indeed has the property (ii) of the theorem.
633	% By Lemma REF,
634	% there exists some \(N' \ge N\) such that for all \(i\ge N'\),
635	% the base-change
636	% \((\pi_*\F(i))|_{S_f} \to (\pi_{S_f})_*\F_{S_f}(i)\) is an isomorphism for
637	% each \(S_f\). Therefore
638	% \(\F_{S_f}\) is flat over \(S_f\) by Lemma REF,
639	% as the direct images \(\pi_*\F(i)\) for all \(i \ge N'\) are locally
640	% free over \(S_f\). Conversely, if \(\phi : T\to S\) is a morphism such
641	% that \(\F_T\) is flat, then the Hilbert polynomial is locally
642	% constant over \(T\). Let \(T_f\) be the open and closed subscheme of \(T\)
643	% where the Hilbert polynomial is \(f\). Clearly, the set
644	% map \(|T_f| \to |S|\) factors via \(|S_f|\). But as the direct images
645	% \({\pi_T}_*\F_T(i)\) are locally free of rank \(f(i)\) on \(T_f\), it
646	% follows in fact that the schematic morphism \(T_f \to S\) factors
647	% via \(S_f\), proving the property (ii) of the theorem."
648	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L2065-L2082.)
649	\begin{proof}
650	  
651	  Flatness of \(i^* \F\) over \(S'\) was established at the end of the proof of
652	  Theorem~REF (the
653	  base-change-without-flatness lemma plus the graded-module-flatness criterion
654	  applied to the coherent sheaves \(\pi_* \F(N+i)\) on each \(S_f\)).
655	  Conversely, if \(\varphi : T \to S\) satisfies \(\varphi^* \F\) on \(\PP^n_T\) is
656	  \(T\)-flat, then \(T\)-flatness of the family forces the Hilbert polynomial to be
657	  locally constant on \(T\).  Decompose \(T = \coprod_f T_f\) into the open-closed
658	  subschemes where the Hilbert polynomial is \(f\).  The set map \(|T_f| \to |S|\)
659	  factors through \(|S_f|\); further, since the direct images
660	  \((\pi_T)_* \varphi^* \F(i)\) are locally-free of rank \(f(i)\) on \(T_f\) for $i
661	  \ge N'\(, the universal property of \)S_f \subseteq W_f$ as the closed
662	  subscheme cut out by these local-freeness conditions
663	  (Lemma~REF) yields a unique scheme
664	  morphism \(T_f \to S_f\) extending the set map. Assembling over \(f\) gives the
665	  unique factorisation \(\varphi = i \circ \tilde \varphi\).
666	\end{proof}

668	\section{Application: relative curves}
669	\label{sec:flatstrat_curve_application}
670	
671	In the Route~A consumer setting, the input to the flattening-stratification
672	machinery is the pullback \(C \times_k T \to T\) where \(C\) is a smooth proper
673	curve over a field \(k\) and \(T\) is any \(k\)-scheme.  The hypotheses of
674	Nitsure's Theorem~REF demand
675	\emph{projective} relative dimension, not merely \emph{proper}; we record that
676	no extra work is required to instantiate the theorem in our setting.
677	
678	\begin{lemma}
679	[Smooth proper curve over \(k\) is projective]
680	  \label{lem:smooth_proper_curve_projective}
681	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6.7 / II.7.6 (curve \(\Rightarrow\) quasi-projective;
682	  % proper \(\Rightarrow\) projective for curves), and standard consequences in
683	  % chapter III. The precise statement ``every smooth proper curve over a field
684	  % is projective'' is one of the classical equivalences for \(\dim 1\) and is
685	  % textbook material; this block is project-bespoke (no verbatim source
686	  % quote is reproduced here as the result is folklore-level and cited only
687	  % structurally for the Route~A reduction).
688	  \textit{Source: classical; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.6.7 \& II.7.6 (curves).}
689	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over a field \(k\).  Then \(C \to \Spec k\) is
690	  projective.  Consequently, for any noetherian \(k\)-scheme \(T\), the relative
691	  curve \(C \times_k T \to T\) is projective in the sense of Grothendieck (there
692	  exists a relatively very ample line bundle on \(C \times_k T\) over \(T\)).
693	\end{lemma}
694	
695	\begin{proof}
696	  Projectivity of a smooth proper curve over a field is classical
697	  ([Hartshorne], II.6.7 / II.7.6; alternatively: every divisor of large
698	  positive degree on a smooth proper curve \(C\) is very ample, providing the
699	  embedding \(C \hookrightarrow \PP^N_k\) for some \(N\)). Pulling the ample line
700	  bundle back along \(C \times_k T \to C\) produces a relatively very ample line
701	  bundle on \(C \times_k T\) over \(T\).
702	\end{proof}
703	
704	\begin{corollary}
705	[Flattening stratification for a coherent sheaf on a relative curve]
706	  \label{cor:flattening_stratification_curve}
707	  
708	  Let \(k\) be a field, \(C\) a smooth proper curve over \(k\), and \(T\) a noetherian
709	  \(k\)-scheme.  Let \(\F\) be a coherent sheaf on \(C \times_k T\). Then the
710	  flattening-stratification conclusion of
711	  Theorem~REF applies: there is a finite
712	  set \(I\) of Hilbert polynomials and locally-closed subschemes
713	  \(T_f \hookrightarrow T\) for \(f \in I\), set-theoretically covering \(T\)
714	  disjointly, such that (a) \(\F|_{C \times_k T_f}\) is flat over \(T_f\) for
715	  each \(f\), and (b) any morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\) such that
716	  \(\varphi^* \F\) on \(C \times_k T'\) is \(T'\)-flat factors uniquely through some
717	  \(T_f\).
718	\end{corollary}
719	
720	\begin{proof}
721	  
722	  By Lemma~REF, \(C \times_k T \to T\) is
723	  projective, hence factors through a closed immersion into \(\PP^n_T\) for some
724	  \(n \ge 0\).  Push \(\F\) forward along the closed immersion (extending by zero
725	  outside the image) to a coherent sheaf \(\tilde \F\) on \(\PP^n_T\); this
726	  operation preserves \(T\)-flatness in both directions because the closed
727	  immersion is a finite morphism.  Apply
728	  Theorem~REF to \(\tilde \F\) on
729	  \(\PP^n_T\) to obtain \(\{T_f\}\) with the stated universal property.
730	\end{proof}
731

733	\label{sec:flatstrat_mathlib_status}
734	
735	Mathlib (master \texttt{b80f227}) provides:
736	\begin{itemize}
737	  \item \texttt{Module.Flat} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Basic}) --- the
738	    module-theoretic notion of flatness, applicable affine-locally to the
739	    stalks \(\F_x\).
740	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Flat} ---
741	    \texttt{IsFlat} as a morphism property and basic stability lemmas; this is
742	    flatness of the structure morphism, \emph{not} flatness of a quasi-coherent
743	    module over the base.
744	  \item \texttt{Module.FaithfullyFlat} and the descent infrastructure that
745	    consumes it.
746	\end{itemize}
747	
748	\noindent What Mathlib does \emph{not} yet have, and that this chapter will
749	introduce in the new file
750	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean}:
751	\begin{itemize}
752	  \item A predicate \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat}
753	    capturing \(S\)-flatness of a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module on a finite-type
754	    \(S\)-scheme (Definition~REF). This is the
755	    sheaf-level lift of \texttt{Module.Flat} via the affine-local
756	    characterization in the second paragraph of the definition.
757	  \item The constructive existence proof
758	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification}
759	    (Theorem~REF) verbatim from
760	    Nitsure~\S4.  The proof decomposes into the three sub-lemmas
761	    \texttt{flatLocusStratification} (Lemma~REF),
762	    \texttt{flatLocusReduction} (Lemma~REF, the
763	    Noetherian-induction reduction via generic flatness), and
764	    \texttt{flatLocusAssembly} (Lemma~REF).
765	  \item The universal-property packaging
766	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification\_universal}
767	    (Theorem~REF) as a
768	    \texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy}-style universal arrow.
769	  \item The relative-curve specialisation
770	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification.ofCurve}
771	    (Corollary~REF) for the Route~A
772	    consumer.
773	\end{itemize}
774	
775	Inputs from Mathlib that the proof consumes:
776	\begin{itemize}
777	  \item \texttt{CategoryTheory.Limits.Cofan} / \texttt{Sigma} for the coproduct
778	    \(\coprod_f S_f\).
779	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocallyClosedImmersion} (and the locally-closed
780	    subscheme API) for the strata \(S_f \hookrightarrow S\).
781	  \item \texttt{IsNoetherian} / \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Noetherian} for the
782	    Noetherian-induction termination argument.
783	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsProjective} (or
784	    \texttt{ProjectiveMorphism}) and the relative-\(\PP^n\) construction, to
785	    state and unpack the projective-morphism hypothesis on
786	    \(\pi : \PP^n_S \to S\).
787	  \item Higher-direct-image base-change lemmas (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry
788	    .Cohomology}, or a Stacks-tag-02KH-shaped lemma; cf.\ the existing
789	    \cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec} use of the same tag) for the \(E_i = \pi_*\F(N+i)\)
790	    construction in Lemma~REF.
791	  \item Castelnuovo--Mumford regularity / Serre vanishing for the
792	    \texttt{N\_1}-existence step.  Mathlib does not yet have these in the
793	    relative form needed; a future sub-lemma may stub them or rely on
794	    \texttt{Module.IsTorsion} arguments on the graded module
795	    \(\bigoplus_{m \ge N} \pi_* \F(m)\).
796	\end{itemize}
797	
798	\section{Out of scope}
799	\label{sec:flatstrat_out_of_scope}
800	
801	This chapter packages \emph{only} the flattening stratification of a coherent
802	sheaf on a projective morphism, plus the relative-curve specialisation. The
803	following downstream / parallel constructions live in separate sibling
804	chapters and are explicitly out of scope here:
805	\begin{itemize}
806	  \item \textbf{Castelnuovo--Mumford regularity} (Nitsure~\S2) --- input to the
807	    Quot construction, used implicitly inside the proof of
808	    Lemma~REF as the assertion that the
809	    Hilbert function stabilises beyond a uniform \(N\).  Its own blueprint home
810	    is the planned chapter \texttt{Picard\_CastelnuovoMumford.tex} (not yet
811	    scaffolded).
812	  \item \textbf{Quot scheme construction} (Nitsure~\S5, A.2.b) --- consumer of
813	    the present chapter; its home is the planned \texttt{Picard\_QuotScheme.tex}.
814	  \item \textbf{Relative Picard functor} (A.1.c) ---
815	    \texttt{Picard\_RelPicFunctor.tex} (planned).
816	  \item \textbf{Picard scheme representability for a smooth proper curve}
817	    (A.2.c) --- the assembly theorem fed by both the Quot construction (which
818	    consumes this chapter) and the relative Picard functor; home is the
819	    planned \texttt{Picard\_FGAPicRepresentability.tex}.
820	\end{itemize}
821

1	\chapter{Relative Spec}
2	\label{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-172). This chapter is the foundational A.1.a sub-row of
6	% Route A (positive-genus arm of nonempty_jacobianWitness). It packages the
7	% relative-spectrum functor \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) : \mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)^{op}
8	% \to \mathrm{Sch}/X used by the relative Picard functor on a product C \times_k T.
9	% Mathlib has the absolute Spec functor and affine gluing (Scheme.GlueData,
10	% AffineScheme.glueOpens) but no general RelativeSpec construction; this chapter is
11	% the prover-ready specification for the new file AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean
12	% which iter-173 (file-skeleton lane) will scaffold.
13	
14	\section{Setup and motivation}
15	\label{sec:relspec_setup}
16	
17	The Route~A construction of \(J = \Pic^0_{C/k}\) as a \(g\)-dimensional abelian variety is
18	organised around the relative Picard functor
19	\(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) = \Pic(C \times_k T) / \pi_T^* \Pic(T)\)
20	on the category of \(k\)-schemes. Representing \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) requires the line-bundle
21	pullback construction on a relative curve \(C \times_k T \to T\), which in turn rests on a
22	\emph{relative scheme} construction on a quasi-coherent sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebras
23	\(\mathcal{A}\), the \emph{relative spectrum}
24	\(\pi : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to X\). This chapter packages the construction
25	of \(\underline{\Spec}_X(-)\) and its three core properties --- universal property, affine
26	base, base change --- as the foundational A.1.a building block. The relative Picard
27	functor itself (A.1.c), the line-bundle pullback functor on a product (A.1.b), and the
28	identity-component / dimension theory of \(J\) (A.3) live in separate sibling chapters
29	(\S~``Out of scope'' below).
30	
31	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Section ``Relative spectrum via glueing'' and ``Relative
32	% spectrum as a functor'' (tags 01LL, 01LO, 01LQ, 01LR, 01LS, available locally at
33	% references/stacks-constructions.tex L309-L720). The Hartshorne reference is
34	% [Hartshorne], II Exercise 5.17 (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf).
35	
36	\section{Quasi-coherent sheaves of algebras}
37	\label{sec:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
38	
39	\begin{definition}\leanok
40	[Quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebra]
41	  \label{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
42	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QcohAlgebra}
43	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Section 01LL ``Relative spectrum via glueing'',
44	  % Situation ``situation-relative-spec''
45	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L312-L318).
46	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
47	  % "Here \(S\) is a scheme, and \(\mathcal{A}\) is a quasi-coherent
48	  %  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-algebra. This means that \(\mathcal{A}\) is a
49	  %  sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-algebras which is quasi-coherent as an
50	  %  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module."
51	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LL (situation-relative-spec).}
52	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. A \emph{quasi-coherent sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebras} is a
53	  sheaf \(\mathcal{A}\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebras (i.e. an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
54	  equipped with \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear multiplication and unit morphisms satisfying
55	  associativity, commutativity, and the unit law as sheaf morphisms) whose underlying
56	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is quasi-coherent. We write \(\mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)\) for the
57	  category of quasi-coherent sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebras and
58	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebra morphisms.
59	\end{definition}
60	
61	% NOTE (iter-179 review): supersedes the iter-174 carrier note. The iter-179
62	% Block A refactor (analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md) upgrades the carrier
63	% to a 3-field structure

65	%     `  sheaf        : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat.{u} X.toPresheafedSpace`
66	%     `  unit         : X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf`
67	%     `  coequifibered : NatTrans.Coequifibered`
68	%     `    (Functor.whiskerLeft (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op unit.hom)`
69	% The new third field IS the iter-175+ overlay the prior note flagged: Mathlib
70	% commit `b80f227`'s Stacks-01LL `NatTrans.Coequifibered` predicate is strictly
71	% weaker than the full `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` claimed by Definition
72	% 1.1's prose but is equivalent for the geometric purpose (constructing the
73	% relative-spectrum scheme) via the dense-subsite equivalence
74	% `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. This is the predicate Mathlib's
75	% `AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData` consumes, so the iter-179
76	% `RelativeSpec`/`structureMorphism` bodies (now real Mathlib values) close
77	% against it.
78	
79	\section{The relative-spectrum scheme}
80	\label{sec:relspec_scheme}
81	
82	The construction is given affine-locally: on every affine open \(U = \Spec R \subseteq X\) one
83	sets \(\pi^{-1}(U) := \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))\) regarded as an \(R\)-algebra, and the local pieces
84	glue compatibly because \(\mathcal{A}\) is quasi-coherent (the transition isomorphism
85	\(\mathcal{A}(U) \otimes_R S \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{A}(V)\) for an inclusion
86	\(V = \Spec S \subseteq U\) gives an open immersion of the corresponding Specs).
87	
88	\begin{theorem}\leanok
89	[Relative spectrum exists]
90	  \label{thm:relative_spec_exists}
91	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec}
92	  
93	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-glue-relative-spec)
94	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L381-L405).
95	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
96	  % "In Situation REF.
97	  %  There exists a morphism of schemes
98	  %  \(\)
99	  %  \pi : \underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A}) \longrightarrow S
100	  %  \(\)
101	  %  with the following properties:
102	  %  \begin{enumerate}
103	  %  \item for every affine open \(U \subset S\) there exists an isomorphism
104	  %  \(i_U : \pi^{-1}(U) \to \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))\) over \(U\), and
105	  %  \item for \(U \subset U' \subset S\) affine open the composition
106	  %  \(\)
107	  %  \xymatrix{
108	  %  \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \ar[r]^{i_U^{-1}} &
109	  %  \pi^{-1}(U) \ar[rr]^{inclusion} & &
110	  %  \pi^{-1}(U') \ar[r]^{i_{U'}} &
111	  %  \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))
112	  %  }
113	  %  \(\)
114	  %  is the open immersion of Lemma REF above.
115	  %  \end{enumerate}
116	  %  Moreover, \(\underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A})\)
117	  %  is unique up to unique isomorphism over \(S\)."
118	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-glue-relative-spec);
119	  cf.\ [Hartshorne], II~Ex.~5.17(a).}
120	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\mathcal{A}\) a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebra
121	  (Definition~REF). There exists a scheme
122	  \(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\) together with an affine morphism
123	  \(\pi : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to X\) such that
124	  \begin{enumerate}
125	    \item for every affine open \(U \subseteq X\), there is an isomorphism
126	      \(i_U : \pi^{-1}(U) \xrightarrow{\sim} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))\) over \(U\);
127	    \item for \(U \subseteq U' \subseteq X\) both affine open, the composite
128	      $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \xrightarrow{i_U^{-1}} \pi^{-1}(U) \hookrightarrow
129	      \pi^{-1}(U') \xrightarrow{i_{U'}} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))$
130	      coincides with the open immersion induced by the restriction
131	      \(\mathcal{A}(U') \to \mathcal{A}(U)\).
132	  \end{enumerate}
133	  The pair \((\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}), \pi)\) is unique up to unique isomorphism
134	  over \(X\).
135	\end{theorem}
136	
137	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Follows immediately from
138	% Lemmas REF,
139	% REF, and
140	% REF.
141	% Uniqueness is stated in the last sentence of
142	% Lemma REF."
143	\begin{proof}
144	  
145	  
146	\leanok
147	  By the open-cover gluing principle for schemes (Mathlib
148	  \texttt{Scheme.GlueData} / \texttt{AffineScheme.glueOpens}) it suffices to specify
149	  affine local pieces \(\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))\) for each affine open \(U \subseteq X\) and
150	  open-immersion transition data
151	  \(\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \hookrightarrow \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))\) for \(U \subseteq U'\)
152	  satisfying the cocycle condition. The transition morphisms come from the ring maps
153	  \(\mathcal{A}(U') \to \mathcal{A}(U)\); quasi-coherence yields the cartesian square
154	  \(\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) = U \times_{U'} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))\), so each transition is an
155	  open immersion. Transitivity (Stacks lemma-transitive-spec) gives the cocycle condition.
156	  Uniqueness of the glued scheme is the standard property of the gluing functor.
157	\end{proof}
158	
159	\begin{theorem}\leanok
160	[Universal property of the relative spectrum]
161	  \label{thm:relative_spec_univ}
162	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty}
163	  
164	  % NOTE (iter-173 review): the iter-173 file-skeleton encodes this as the
165	  % structural consequence ``IsAffineHom (structureMorphism 𝒜)'' rather than
166	  % the natural-bijection statement pinned by this prose. Iter-174+ must
167	  % upgrade the Lean type to a ``CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy''
168	  % witness (or equivalent) before any consumer file relies on this theorem.
169	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ continuation: Section ``Relative spectrum as
170	  % a functor'', functor F and Lemma lemma-spec
171	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L427-L466 + L547-L551).
172	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
173	  % "For any \(f : T \to S\) the pullback
174	  %  \(f^*\mathcal{A}\) is a quasi-coherent sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-algebras.
175	  %  We are going to consider pairs \((f : T \to S, \varphi)\) where
176	  %  \(f\) is a morphism of schemes and \(\varphi : f^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T\)
177	  %  is a morphism of \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-algebras. Note that this is the
178	  %  same as giving a \(f^{-1}\mathcal{O}_S\)-algebra homomorphism
179	  %  \(\varphi : f^{-1}\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T\), see
180	  %  Sheaves, Lemma REF.
181	  %  This is also the same as giving an \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-algebra map
182	  %  \(\varphi : \mathcal{A} \to f_*\mathcal{O}_T\), see
183	  %  Sheaves, Lemma REF.
184	  %  We will use all three ways of thinking about \(\varphi\),
185	  %  without further mention. [...]
186	  %  In this way we have defined a functor
187	  %  \begin{eqnarray}
188	  %  \label{equation-spec}
189	  %  F : \Sch^{opp} & \longrightarrow & \textit{Sets} \\
190	  %  T & \longmapsto & F(T) = \{\text{pairs }(f, \varphi) \text{ as above}\}
191	  %  \nonumber
192	  %  \end{eqnarray}
193	  %  [...]
194	  %  The functor \(F\) is representable by a scheme."
195	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-spec + section-spec
196	  functor description); [Hartshorne], II~Ex.~5.17(b).}
197	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\mathcal{A}\) a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebra.
198	  Then \(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\) represents the functor
199	  \(F : \Sch^{op} \to \mathrm{Set}\),
200	  $T \mapsto F(T) := \{(f, \varphi) : f : T \to X \text{ a morphism},\;
201	  \varphi : f^* \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T \text{ an } \mathcal{O}_T\text{-algebra map}\}.$
202	  Equivalently, for any \(X\)-scheme \(g : T \to X\),
203	  there is a natural bijection
204	  \[
205	    \Hom_X(T, \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})) \;\;\cong\;\;
206	    \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X\text{-alg}}(\mathcal{A}, g_* \mathcal{O}_T)
207	  \]
208	  (using the adjunction between \(g_*\) and \(g^*\) to convert between
209	  \(\mathcal{A} \to g_*\mathcal{O}_T\) and \(g^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T\)).
210	  The bijection is natural in \(T\).
211	\end{theorem}

214	% (proof of lemma-spec, L553-L600, ~50 lines covering the Zariski sheaf property,
215	% the subfunctors F_i associated to an affine cover, and identifying each F_i
216	% with the affine-base case via lemma-spec-base-change and lemma-spec-affine).
217	% Verbatim quote omitted for length; the structural steps are restated below in
218	% project notation.
219	\begin{proof}
220	  
221	  \leanok
222	  % NOTE (iter-179 review): supersedes the iter-176 placeholder-discharge note.
223	  % The iter-179 Block A refactor replaces the iter-176 placeholder bodies with
224	  % the Mathlib values ``(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData _).glued`` /
225	  % ``.toBase``; iter-179 Lane B closes the Lean ``UniversalProperty`` proof
226	  % via ``isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen`` +
227	  % ``relativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι`` +
228	  % ``isAffineOpen_opensRange`` — a real proof against the gluing infrastructure,
229	  % NOT against a trivial identity placeholder. The Lean type remains the
230	  % strictly weaker ``IsAffineHom (structureMorphism 𝒜)'' rather than the
231	  % Yoneda-bijection form pinned above (iter-174+ commitment; still pending
232	  % iter-180+). Consumers may now rely on the affineness conclusion, but the
233	  % Yoneda-bijection witness is still deferred.
234	  Following Stacks tag 01LQ (proof of lemma-spec): the functor \(F\) is a Zariski sheaf
235	  because morphisms of schemes and \(\mathcal{O}\)-algebra maps both glue under open covers.
236	  Choose an affine open cover \(X = \bigcup U_i\). The subfunctor
237	  \(F_i \subseteq F\) of pairs \((f, \varphi)\) with \(f(T) \subseteq U_i\) is representable by
238	  \(\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U_i))\) via the affine-base case
239	  (Theorem~REF) together with the
240	  base-change identification \(F_i \cong F_{U_i,\,\mathcal{A}|_{U_i}}\)
241	  (Theorem~REF). Each \(F_i \hookrightarrow F\) is an open
242	  subfunctor (pull back \(U_i\) along \(f\)). The \(F_i\) jointly cover \(F\) since the \(U_i\)
243	  cover \(X\). The gluing of representable open subfunctors produces a representing scheme,
244	  which is canonically isomorphic to the glued scheme \(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\)
245	  of Theorem~REF.
246	\end{proof}

248	\section{Affine base}
249	\label{sec:relspec_affine}
250	
251	\begin{theorem}\leanok
252	[Relative spectrum over an affine base]
253	  \label{thm:relative_spec_affine_base}
254	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.affine_base_iff}
255	  % NOTE (iter-173 review): the iter-173 file-skeleton encodes this as the
256	  % weaker ``IsAffine ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜)'' rather than the canonical
257	  % iso ``Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))'' pinned by this prose. The Lean name
258	  % ``affine_base_iff'' is also misleading (the encoded type is not an iff).
259	  % Iter-174+ refines to the iff-with-Γ statement once Γ : QcohAlgebra → CommRingCat
260	  % is available.
261	  
262	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LO (lemma-spec-affine)
263	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L491-L545).
264	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
265	  % "In Situation REF.
266	  %  Let \(F\) be the functor associated to \((S, \mathcal{A})\) above.
267	  %  If \(S\) is affine, then \(F\) is representable by the
268	  %  affine scheme \(\Spec(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{A}))\)."
269	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LO (lemma-spec-affine).}
270	  Let \(X = \Spec R\) be an affine scheme and let \(\mathcal{A}\) be a quasi-coherent
271	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebra. Set \(A := \Gamma(X, \mathcal{A})\), regarded as an
272	  \(R\)-algebra. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of \(X\)-schemes
273	  \[
274	    \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \;\;\cong\;\; \Spec(A).
275	  \]
276	  In particular, on an affine base the relative spectrum reduces to the absolute
277	  spectrum of the global sections.
278	\end{theorem}
279	
280	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Write \(S = \Spec(R)\) and \(A = \Gamma(S, \mathcal{A})\).
281	% Then \(A\) is an \(R\)-algebra and \(\mathcal{A} = \widetilde A\).
282	% The ring map \(R \to A\) gives rise to a canonical map
283	% \(\)
284	% f_{univ} : \Spec(A)
285	% \longrightarrow
286	% S = \Spec(R).
287	% \(\)
288	% We have
289	% \(f_{univ}^*\mathcal{A} =  \widetilde{A \otimes_R A}\)
290	% by Schemes, Lemma REF.
291	% Hence there is a canonical map
292	% \(\)
293	% \varphi_{univ} :
294	% f_{univ}^*\mathcal{A} = \widetilde{A \otimes_R A}
295	% \longrightarrow
296	% \widetilde A = \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(A)}
297	% \(\)
298	% coming from the \(A\)-module map \(A \otimes_R A \to A\),
299	% \(a \otimes a' \mapsto aa'\). We claim that the pair
300	% \((f_{univ}, \varphi_{univ})\) represents \(F\) in this case."
301	\begin{proof}
302	  
303	  \leanok
304	  % NOTE (iter-179 review): supersedes the iter-176 placeholder-discharge note.
305	  % The iter-176 ``change IsAffine (Spec R); inferInstance`` form is now stale
306	  % — iter-179 Lane B closes via ``UniversalProperty 𝒜`` + ``isAffine_of_isAffineHom``
307	  % against the iter-179 Mathlib-backed ``RelativeSpec``/``structureMorphism``
308	  % bodies. The Lean type remains the strictly weaker ``IsAffine`` rather than
309	  % the canonical iso ``Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X,𝒜))'' named below (iter-174+
310	  % commitment; still pending iter-180+).
311	  Write \(X = \Spec R\) and \(A = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{A})\). Quasi-coherence of
312	  \(\mathcal{A}\) gives \(\mathcal{A} = \widetilde{A}\) as \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
313	  (with the \(R\)-algebra structure on \(A\) inducing the algebra structure on
314	  \(\widetilde A\)). The ring map \(R \to A\) gives a morphism
315	  \(f_{\mathrm{univ}} : \Spec A \to \Spec R = X\) and a canonical
316	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A}\)-algebra map
317	  $\varphi_{\mathrm{univ}} : f_{\mathrm{univ}}^* \mathcal{A} = \widetilde{A \otimes_R A}
318	  \to \mathcal{O}_{\Spec A} = \widetilde A$ given by multiplication
319	  \(a \otimes a' \mapsto aa'\). We verify \((\Spec A, f_{\mathrm{univ}}, \varphi_{\mathrm{univ}})\)
320	  represents \(F\) on the affine base: given a test scheme \(T\) and a pair
321	  \((f, \varphi)\), the composite
322	  $A = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{A}) \xrightarrow{f^\sharp}
323	  \Gamma(T, f^*\mathcal{A}) \xrightarrow{\varphi} \Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T)$
324	  is a ring map \(A \to \Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T)\) and hence corresponds to a morphism
325	  \(T \to \Spec A\) by the affine universal property
326	  (Mathlib \texttt{Scheme.toSpec\_naturality} /
327	  \texttt{Scheme.Hom.toSpec}). One checks this assignment inverts the universal map
328	  \((f, \varphi) \mapsto a\) defined by \(f = f_{\mathrm{univ}} \circ a\),
329	  \(\varphi = a^* \varphi_{\mathrm{univ}}\).
330	\end{proof}

349	  % "In Situation REF.

352	  %  Let \(g : S' \to S\) be a morphism of schemes.

365	  Let \(g : T \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and let \(\mathcal{A}\) be a quasi-coherent

374	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "A pair \((f' : T \to S', \varphi' : (f')^*\mathcal{A}' \to \mathcal{O}_T)\)
375	% is the same as a pair \((f, \varphi : f^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T)\)
376	% together with a factorization of \(f\) as \(f = g \circ f'\). Namely with
377	% this notation we have
378	% \((f')^* \mathcal{A}' = (f')^*g^*\mathcal{A} = f^*\mathcal{A}\).
379	% Hence the lemma."
380	\begin{proof}
381	  
382	  \leanok
383	  % NOTE (iter-185 review, supersedes iter-179): the iter-185 Lane D HARD-BAR
384	  % closed BOTH iter-179 Mathlib-gap sorries kernel-clean:
385	  % (i) ``Scheme.QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered`` (the ``coequifibered``
386	  % field, RelativeSpec.lean L358-377) via
387	  % ``coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway`` +
388	  % ``IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen``;
389	  % (ii) ``Scheme.RelativeSpec.pullback_iso_desc_isIso`` (the canonical iso body,
390	  % RelativeSpec.lean L546-632) via the explicit 3-iso chain
391	  % (hPre identity \(\to\) isoOpensRange.symm \(\to\) pullback_iso_affine_piece.symm)
392	  % and ``IsZariskiLocalAtTarget`` affineness criterion.
393	  % The Lean type remains the weaker existential rather than the canonical iso
394	  % with a *named* pullback ``g^*𝒜'' pinned by this prose (iter-174+ signature
395	  % refinement still pending — see chapter NOTE on the statement block above).
396	  % Consumers may now consume ``pullback_iso''s witness as a fully axiom-clean
397	  % closure (no more "load-bearing-pending" qualifier); A.1.a body-level work
398	  % is functionally complete.
399	  By Theorem~REF, \(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\) represents
400	  the functor \(F\) sending an \(X\)-scheme \(h : S \to X\) to the set of
401	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-algebra maps \(h^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_S\). A morphism
402	  \(S \to T \times_X \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\) over \(T\) is the same as a pair of an
403	  \(X\)-morphism \(S \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\) together with a \(T\)-factorisation
404	  of \(S \to X\), i.e.\ a morphism \(S \to T\) whose composition with \(g\) equals
405	  \(S \to X\). Tracing through, this is a \(T\)-morphism \(f' : S \to T\) together with an
406	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-algebra map \((f')^*(g^*\mathcal{A}) = f^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_S\).
407	  This is precisely \(F'(S)\), the functor for \((T, g^*\mathcal{A})\), so the canonical map
408	  \(T \times_X \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to \underline{\Spec}_T(g^*\mathcal{A})\)
409	  is an isomorphism by Yoneda.

415	\begin{theorem}\leanok
416	[Relative spectrum as a functor]
417	  \label{thm:relative_spec_functorial}
418	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.functor}
419	  
420	  % NOTE (iter-173 review): the iter-173 file-skeleton encodes this as a
421	  % bare object-level function ``QcohAlgebra → Over X'' (not a categorical
422	  % ``Functor''), with morphism action + functoriality + the equivalence
423	  % onto affine X-schemes all deferred. Iter-174+ upgrades to the full
424	  % contravariant ``Functor (X.QcohAlgebra)ᵒᵖ (Over X)'' with the universal
425	  % property-induced ``map'' once QcohAlgebra is unpacked.
426	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LR (definition-relative-spec +
427	  % lemma-glueing-gives-functor-spec + lemma-spec-properties part (3))
428	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L602-L691).
429	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
430	  % "Let \(S\) be a scheme. Let \(\mathcal{A}\) be a quasi-coherent sheaf of
431	  %  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-algebras. The {\it relative spectrum of \(\mathcal{A}\) over
432	  %  \(S\)}, or simply the {\it spectrum of \(\mathcal{A}\) over \(S\)} is the scheme
433	  %  constructed in Lemma REF which represents the
434	  %  functor \(F\) (REF), see
435	  %  Lemma REF.
436	  %  We denote it \(\pi : \underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A}) \to S\).
437	  %  The ``universal family'' is a morphism of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-algebras
438	  %  \(\)
439	  %  \mathcal{A}
440	  %  \longrightarrow
441	  %  \pi_*\mathcal{O}_{\underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A})}
442	  %  \(\)
443	  %  [...]
444	  %  The universal map
445	  %  \(\)
446	  %  \mathcal{A}
447	  %  \longrightarrow
448	  %  \pi_*\mathcal{O}_{\underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A})}
449	  %  \(\)
450	  %  is an isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-algebras."
451	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LR (definition-relative-spec +
452	  lemma-glueing-gives-functor-spec + lemma-spec-properties);
453	  [Hartshorne], II~Ex.~5.17(b).}
454	  The construction \(\mathcal{A} \mapsto \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\) assembles into
455	  a contravariant functor
456	  \[
457	    \underline{\Spec}_X : \mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathrm{AffSch}/X
458	  \]
459	  from quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebras to affine \(X\)-schemes: a morphism of
460	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebras \(\psi : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}\) induces, by the universal
461	  property (Theorem~REF) applied to the pair
462	  $(\pi_\mathcal{B}, \psi \circ \pi_\mathcal{B}^*) :
463	  \pi_\mathcal{B}^*\mathcal{A} \to \pi_\mathcal{B}^*\mathcal{B} \to \mathcal{O}_{\Spec\mathcal{B}}$,
464	  an \(X\)-morphism
465	  \(\underline{\Spec}_X(\psi) : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{B}) \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\).
466	  Functoriality (identity, composition) is direct from the universal property. The
467	  pushforward \(\pi_* \mathcal{O}_{\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})} \cong \mathcal{A}\)
468	  exhibits \(\underline{\Spec}_X\) as an equivalence between \(\mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)^{op}\) and
469	  the full subcategory of affine \(X\)-schemes, with inverse \(f \mapsto f_* \mathcal{O}_Y\)
470	  (Stacks lemma-spec-properties part~(3)).

473	\begin{proof}
474	  
475	  \leanok
476	  Functoriality follows directly from Theorem~REF: for
477	  \(\psi : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}\), the pair
478	  $(\pi_\mathcal{B} : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{B}) \to X, \;
479	  \pi_\mathcal{B}^*(\psi))\( defines an \)X$-morphism
480	  \(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{B}) \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\) via the
481	  representing universal pair. Identity and composition follow from naturality of the
482	  representing bijection in \(\mathcal{A}\). For the equivalence claim, \(\underline{\Spec}_X\)
483	  is fully faithful because the isomorphism
484	  \(\pi_* \mathcal{O}_{\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})} \cong \mathcal{A}\)
485	  (locally identifying with \(\Gamma(\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)),\mathcal{O}) = \mathcal{A}(U)\)
486	  via Theorem~REF) reconstructs \(\mathcal{A}\) from
487	  \(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\). Essential surjectivity onto affine \(X\)-schemes uses
488	  the canonical morphism \(X \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\pi_*\mathcal{O}_X)\) of Stacks
489	  lemma-canonical-morphism; on an affine \(X\)-scheme \(\pi : Y \to X\), this canonical
490	  morphism is an isomorphism (affine-locally it is the identity
491	  \(\Spec\Gamma(\pi^{-1}(U), \mathcal{O}_Y) \to \Spec\Gamma(\pi^{-1}(U), \mathcal{O}_Y)\)).
492	\end{proof}

494	\section{Lean encoding}
495	\label{sec:relspec_lean_encoding}
496	
497	The Lean target file is the new \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean}.
498	The construction proceeds by gluing absolute affine pieces: for each affine open
499	\(U \subseteq X\) with \(U = \Spec R\) and \(A := \mathcal{A}(U)\) regarded as an
500	\(R\)-algebra, the local piece is \(\Spec A\) with structure morphism \(\Spec A \to \Spec R = U\)
501	induced by the algebra unit \(R \to A\). Mathlib provides \texttt{IsAffineOpen.toScheme} for
502	the affine-open inclusion and the absolute \texttt{Scheme.Spec} functor for the local
503	pieces; the transition isomorphisms come from quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{A}\) via
504	\texttt{QuasiCoherent.tensorIso} (or the explicit
505	\(\mathcal{A}(U) \otimes_R S \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{A}(V)\) for \(V = \Spec S \subseteq U\)).
506	The gluing backbone is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData} (general construction) or
507	the convenience wrapper \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme.glueOpens}; the cocycle
508	condition reduces to Stacks \texttt{lemma-transitive-spec}. The universal property
509	(Theorem~REF) is expressed as a
510	\texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy} witness, providing the natural bijection
511	$\Hom_X(T, \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})) \cong
512	\Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X\text{-alg}}(\mathcal{A}, g_*\mathcal{O}_T)$ as a
513	\texttt{Yoneda}-style structure. The base-change isomorphism
514	(Theorem~REF) and functoriality
515	(Theorem~REF) are derived from the universal property by
516	Yoneda. No new core axiom is required; the entire chapter is a definitional/structural
517	construction on top of existing Mathlib affine-scheme infrastructure.
518

531	  \item \textbf{Connectedness, dimensionality, smoothness of \(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\)}

534	  \item \textbf{Identity-component \(\Pic^0\) and abelian-variety structure} (A.3) --- a

35	\section{Setup and motivation}
36	\label{sec:relpic_setup}
37	
38	Fix a base field \(k\) and a smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C\) over
39	\(k\). As in \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}, write
40	\(\pi_T : C \times_k T \to T\) for the projection of a relative curve over a
41	\(k\)-scheme \(T\) and
42	\(\pi_T^* : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) for the induced pullback of
43	isomorphism classes of line bundles (\cref{def:pullback_along_projection}).
44	
45	The set-valued relative Picard presheaf
46	\[
47	  \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) \;\;=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, \pi_T^*\Pic(T)
48	\]
49	of \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} was set up forgetting the
50	abelian-group structure inherited from tensor product. This chapter restores
51	that structure and then sheafifies. The first half (\S~REF
52	and \S~REF) verifies that the quotient is canonically
53	an abelian group and the functoriality of \cref{thm:pullback_natural} respects
54	that group structure; the result is a group-valued presheaf
55	\(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k} : (\Sch/k)^{op} \to \Ab\) on the category of \(k\)-schemes. The
56	second half (\S~REF) takes the \'etale sheafification
57	\(\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et} := (\Pic^\sharp_{C/k})^\sim\) in the \'etale topology on
58	\((\Sch/k)^{op}\), and verifies that sheafification preserves the abelian-group
59	target. The \'etale-sheafified functor is the object represented by the Picard
60	scheme in \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}.
61	
62	\section{Group structure on the relative Picard quotient}
63	\label{sec:relpic_group}
64	
65	The Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) of any scheme is canonically an abelian group under
66	tensor product of line bundles (Stacks tag 01CR; the inverse of \([\mathcal{L}]\)
67	is $[\mathcal{L}^{-1}] = [\mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L},
68	\mathcal{O}_X)]$). The pullback map
69	\(\pi_T^* : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) of
70	\cref{def:pullback_along_projection} respects this structure: pullback of
71	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules sends \(\mathcal{O}_T\) to \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\)
72	and is multiplicative on tensor products.
73	
74	\begin{lemma}\leanok
75	[Group structure descends to the relative Picard quotient]
76	  \label{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
77	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup}
78	  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product, def:pullback_along_projection,
79	    thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}
80	
81	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
82	  % Definition df:aPf + Definition df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard
83	  % functors are \emph{abelian-group}-valued, by construction; the relative
84	  % Picard functor is defined as a quotient \emph{group})
85	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
86	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
87	  % "The {\it absolute Picard functor} \(\Pic_X\) is the functor from  the
88	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) \(S\)-schemes \(T\) to the category of
89	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
90	  %     \(\)\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).\(\)
91	  %  [\dots]
92	  %  \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
93	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} \(\Pic_{X/S}\) is defined by
94	  %     \(\)\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).\(\)"
95	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
96	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the Picard functors are abelian-group-valued, and the
97	  relative one is a quotient of abelian groups; cf.\ Stacks tag 01CR for the
98	  abelian-group structure on \(\Pic\)).}
99	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve and let \(T\) be a
100	  \(k\)-scheme. The Picard group \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is an abelian group under
101	  tensor product of line bundles, and the pullback map
102	  \[
103	    \pi_T^* \;\colon\; \Pic(T) \;\longrightarrow\; \Pic(C \times_k T)
104	  \]
105	  of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} is a group homomorphism. Its image
106	  \[
107	    H_T \;\;:=\;\; \pi_T^* \Pic(T)
108	  \]
109	  is therefore a subgroup of \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\), and the quotient set of
110	  \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}
111	  \[
112	    \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) \;\;=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, H_T
113	  \]
114	  inherits a canonical abelian-group structure under which the quotient map
115	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) is a surjective
116	  homomorphism with kernel exactly \(H_T\).
117	\end{lemma}
